title: eriogonum parvifolium var. paynei
intro: Eriogonum parvifolium var. paynei, also known as Payne's buckwheat or Payne's desert buckwheat, is a perennial herb in the buckwheat family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its roots and leaves, which have been used to treat various health issues. The roots and leaves of Eriogonum parvifolium var. paynei contain tannins, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties and health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and supporting digestive health. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or salve for its medicinal uses, and it can be found in various herbal preparations, including capsules, tinctures, and creams.
